← back to brain probe

Iterative Loop: Does Chaining img2img Climb the Brain Score?

In the first probe we ran one pass of img2img and scored the result. Here we close the loop: feed each generated image back as input and repeat 4 times, scoring every iteration through TRIBE v2 on a local Quadro P4000. The question: is the diffusion model a reliable optimizer, or does it overshoot and drift?

Finding
Neither video monotonically improves. Video C peaks at iter 2 (+0.427) then decays to +0.173 by iter 4. Video A climbs slowly with a dip at iter 3, still negative after 4 iterations. The diffusion model is not a gradient ascender — it has its own distributional gravity that can pull an image away from a high-scoring configuration as easily as toward one. The implication: blind chaining is not the right strategy. Score each generation and keep the best, rather than always using the last output as the next input.

What this means for the stronger signal question
What works
Single-step img2img with a brain-optimized prompt already produces large gains. The first probe run got Video C to +0.504 in one step; iteration only matched that at iter 2. Speed and seed variance mean you can often get a better result by running 4 independent single-step generations and picking the highest scorer.
What the images show
The pseudo-text/handwriting that Kev noticed — marks that look like they should be readable but aren't — appears strongest at the peak-scoring frames (Video C iter 2). By iter 4, the model has drifted toward more "stock photo desk" aesthetics with less decipherable ambiguity. The illegibility itself may be load-bearing.
Next experiment
Generate N candidates at each step, score all, keep the best — a proper selection loop. Separately: test whether a prompt that explicitly requests pseudo-text/illegible annotations scores higher than one requesting clean readable diagrams. The gibberish-vs-clean hypothesis is testable in a single ComfyUI + TRIBE run.
Experiment run 2026-06-10 · TRIBE v2 (Meta FAIR) · DreamShaper 8 · denoise=0.55 each step · lil-blue GTX 1060 3GB · 7a72 Quadro P4000 · single-step probe · full brain report
